pages.text: Throw a $(item)Skyseed$() into open air and, after a moment, a brand-new floating island germinates where it settles.$(br2)Craft seeds, harvest islands, craft better seeds, and rise.

$(bold)Throwing$()$(br)Hold right-click to wind up, then release. A quick tap lobs the island in close; a long hold flings it far.$(br2)It arms for about two seconds, then grows in. An island will never grow into another.$(br2)Height matters too: some islands — the mining ones especially — form differently up high than deep down, so aim with care.

$(bold)A living world$()$(br)An island's look depends on the biome it lands in. The same Forest Skyseed grows oak in a forest, acacia in a savanna, sparse grass in plains, and dry dirt and sand in the desert.

$(bold)How this book works$()$(br)It unfolds as you play. Most pages appear once you've crafted the island before them; the rarer ones — farms, ruins, monuments — stay a locked $(bold)?$() until you $(bold)find the item they call for$() (a feather, a totem, a sponge). A page then shows $(bold)what to gather$(), reveals its $(bold)recipe$() when you hold the makings, and unlocks its $(bold)field notes$() the moment you craft it.$(br2)Start with the $(item)Forest Skyseed$() and follow the trail.
